text:
If there's also other background chatter we also need to make the agent aware of we should include it:


<tenex-edge>
Activity on #channel since you last looked:

<@pablo - 5m ago> hey guys
<@agent1 - 3m ago> Good morning!
<@agent2 - 2m ago> I'm here, wassup
<@pablo - just now> @developer, hello! [...] **④ ambient chatter** (tmux and hooks, identical) — framed, FYI, no reply forced:
```
<tenex-edge>
Activity on #tenex-edge since you last looked:

<@pablo - 5m ago> hey guys
<@agent1 - 3m ago> Good morning!
<@agent2 - 2m ago> I'm here, wassup
<@pablo - just now> @developer, hello!
</tenex-edge>
``` [...] A third, structural fact removes a quadrant entirely: **ambient chatter is never pasted into a tmux pane** (it would force the agent to "answer" things it wasn't asked). Today the tmux path only delivers direct mentions; ambient always arrives through the hook context as framed FYI. Keep that — it's correct, and it means "ambient in tmux" and "ambient in hooks" are the *same* block. [...] **④ ambient chatter** (tmux and hooks, identical) — framed, FYI, no reply forced:
``` [...] 5. **Wire `<@name - Xm ago>` prefixes** in ④ (you already have `relative_time` + `format_local_datetime` in `util`); show the relative-time suffix only when older than ~2 min so fresh lines stay clean. [...] 5. yeah, only when older
